mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in PAYEMS signals expanding household income and resilient corporate revenues; higher payroll momentum drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:PAYEMS
fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot was 137622 as of one month before 2007-04-15
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with continued job growth
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month PAYEMS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward triggered observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5% raises equity discount rates and tightens financing conditions; this monetary restraint drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0% and latest monthly value remains above 5.0%
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot was 5.26 as of one month before 2007-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS above 5.0% is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Falling HOUST under high FEDFUNDS identifies a credit-sensitive housing contraction; the joint deterioration reduces construction demand, bank credit quality, and cyclicals, driving negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% over 6 months and FEDFUNDS is above 5.0%
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:HOUST latest snapshot was 1525 as of two months before 2007-04-15
fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot was 5.26 as of one month before 2007-04-15
regime_hint: housing downturn during restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger produces positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations or if HOUST decline alone has the same predictive power as the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating RSAFS indicates resilient consumer spending and near-term revenue support for listed firms; retail-sales strength drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:RSAFS latest snapshot was 367539 as of two months before 2007-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS 3-month growth above 1.0% is not followed by above-median 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE from a low level signals labor-market deterioration before earnings downgrades; a 3-month unemployment increase drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses upward by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:UNRATE latest snapshot was 4.4 as of one month before 2007-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE increases greater than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months are followed by nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or hit-rate for negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS is already high creates an inflation-restraint squeeze: policy cannot ease and margins face cost pressure, driving negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above 3.0% and FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot was 203.906 as of two months before 2007-04-15
fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot was 5.26 as of one month before 2007-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS joint trigger has a nonnegative mean 20-day return or fails to underperform periods with high FEDFUNDS but non-accelerating CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: Positive INDPRO momentum signals stronger real output and capacity utilization, supporting operating leverage and earnings revisions; higher industrial production momentum drives positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:INDPRO latest snapshot was 113.1433 as of two months before 2007-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum is followed by below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations or rolling Rank IC is negative for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating GDPC1 combined with falling HOUST identifies a broad demand slowdown led by residential investment; this interaction drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth crosses below 2.5% and HOUST falls more than 10% year over year
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1
series:HOUST
fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot was 11513.0 as of six months before 2007-04-15
fact:HOUST latest snapshot was 1525 as of two months before 2007-04-15
regime_hint: growth slowdown with housing-led demand contraction
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1 and HOUST slowdown trigger does not produce lower average 60-day S&P 500 returns than unconditional returns or if the negative-return hit-rate is below 55%.
